name: A/B Test
definition: A controlled experiment that presents two or more variants of a feature to different user groups simultaneously and measures behavioral differences. A/B tests generate evidence for product decisions by comparing metrics between a control group and one or more treatment groups. In Atono, A/B tests are enabled by using feature flags with customer-based slice targeting to route different users to different variants.

name: Acceptance Criteria
definition: Testable conditions that define when a story is complete, written as a hierarchical list with up to three levels of nesting. Also called ACs. Each top-level criterion can have sub-criteria and leaf-level criteria. Criteria support inline comments anchored to specific text, attachments, and persistent URLs. When a story is split, acceptance criteria are preserved based on whether parent criteria are moved with all their children. Acceptance criteria are versioned and any edit appears in the story's Activities log.

name: Acceptance Rate
definition: The percentage of stories or acceptance criteria that pass review on the first attempt without requiring rework or rejection. A high acceptance rate indicates that stories are well-defined before development begins and that implementation matches what was specified. Teams track acceptance rate as a quality indicator, often pairing it with cycle time to understand both speed and quality of delivery.

name: Acceptance Test
definition: A test that validates a story meets its acceptance criteria from the user's perspective. Acceptance tests are typically written alongside acceptance criteria and run before a story is marked as done. They may be manual or automated and are distinct from unit or integration tests, focusing on user-observable behavior rather than internal implementation.

name: Active Sprint
definition: The one sprint a Scrum team is currently executing toward a sprint goal. Only one sprint can be active per team at any given time. Active sprint items appear on the In Progress page, contribute to burndown charts, and count toward velocity calculations when the sprint is completed. Starting a new sprint without closing the previous one is not permitted.

name: Activities
definition: A chronological, filterable log of all changes to a story, bug, epic, or feature flag including title changes, workflow transitions, assignments, comments, attachments, subtasks, and pull requests. Each entry shows who performed the action and when. Activities can be filtered by type or source (Atono, Integration, API, MCP Server).

name: Administrator
definition: A workspace role with full administrative privileges including managing workspace settings, environments, users, integrations, and SSO configuration, but without access to billing. Administrators can assign other Administrators and Product Managers but cannot remove their own role.

name: Affected Environment
definition: One or more environments where a bug has been observed, selected from the workspace's configured environments such as development, staging, or production. Used to scope bug impact and prioritize fixes.

name: AI Context
definition: A structured section on a story or bug where AI tools store and retrieve context across work sessions, divided into Design Decisions, Technical Investigation, and Technical Changes. Created and updated through the Atono MCP Server or manually in the Atono web application. Each section has independent version history.

name: API Endpoint
definition: A specific URL path in the Atono public API representing a resource or operation, such as listing stories, creating a bug, or fetching a workspace's environments. Each endpoint accepts defined HTTP methods (GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE) and requires authentication via an API key. Endpoint documentation is available in the Atono API reference.

name: API Key
definition: A user-specific credential for authenticating with the Atono public API, created and managed from the user's profile. Each key has a unique name for identification, tracks last usage, and can be deleted to revoke access. Actions taken with an API key appear as performed by that user account.

name: Archive
definition: Moving inactive, completed, or outdated items out of active views without permanently deleting them. Archived items remain accessible through search or dedicated archive views but do not appear in standard backlogs, filters, or reports. Archiving keeps active workspaces focused on current work while preserving historical context.

name: Ask Capy
definition: An AI-powered Q&A tool that searches completed stories and bugs in your workspace to answer product questions. Requires at least 10 completed items to activate. Each workspace gets 50 questions per day; the counter resets at midnight Eastern time. Every answer includes the source items it was drawn from.

name: Assignee
definition: The user responsible for completing a story or bug. Assignment triggers optional email notifications and helps track ownership. Items can be unassigned (No assignee) or reassigned to different users.

name: Assumption
definition: A stated belief held as true for planning or scoping purposes, pending full validation. Assumptions reduce uncertainty enough to make decisions but should be tracked and revisited as work progresses. If an assumption proves false, it may become a risk or require a change request. Assumptions are often documented in story descriptions or design decisions sections.

name: Atono API
definition: The public REST API for programmatic access to Atono data and operations, authenticated using API Keys. Used by custom tools, automations, and the MCP Server.

name: Atono Chrome Extension
definition: A Chrome browser extension that enables bug reporting with automatic context capture, feature flag flipping, and click mapping for usage tracking directly from your application. Requires meta tags on your application pages to identify the environment and optionally the customer.

name: Atono MCP Server
definition: A local Docker-based server implementing the Model Context Protocol that connects AI development tools like Cursor, Claude, or VS Code to an Atono workspace. Allows AI tools to read and update stories, bugs, workflows, and timeboxes using authenticated workspace access. Updates are marked with a diamond icon in Activities.

name: Attachment
definition: A file added to a story or bug, displayed as thumbnails for images/videos or as file info for other types. Can be attached inline to specific acceptance criteria or to the general attachments area. Limited to 100 files per item, 50 MB per file, with workspace-level storage limits based on plan. Attachment URLs expire after a short time and must be re-fetched.

name: Audit Log
definition: A chronological, tamper-evident record of significant user and system actions across a workspace, such as user invitations, permission changes, SSO configuration updates, and integration connections. Audit logs provide accountability and support security investigations. They are accessible to Workspace Owners and Administrators.

name: Backlog (Page)
definition: A team page for planning and prioritizing upcoming work before it moves into execution. For Scrum teams, organized around sprints with projected capacity. For Kanban teams, displays all 'To do' items beyond the 20 shown in In Progress. Supports list view with filters, bulk actions, and right-click quick actions.

name: Backlog Management
definition: A team setting that, when enabled, designates specific backlog owners as the only users authorized to add, create, reorder, delete, or move stories in the To do and Won't do workflow categories. For Scrum teams, backlog management also controls who can plan and modify sprint scope. When disabled, any user with team access can manage the backlog freely. Up to 10 backlog owners can be assigned to a team.

name: Backlog Owner
definition: A team role responsible for managing a team's planned work when Backlog Management is enabled. Can add, create, reorder, delete stories, and for Scrum teams, manage sprint planning. Does not need to be a team member. A team can have up to 10 backlog owners.

name: Baseline
definition: A fixed reference snapshot of a project's original scope, estimates, or schedule used as a comparison point to measure change over time. Baselines help teams understand how much scope has grown (scope creep), whether cycle times are improving, and how well initial estimates held up. In planning discussions, 'baseline' often refers to the original sprint commitment before any additions or removals.

name: Blocker
definition: An impediment preventing a story or bug from progressing through the workflow. Blockers can be internal (a missing decision, an unresolved dependency) or external (a third-party outage, waiting on another team). Identified in daily standups, blockers should be escalated and resolved by the team or Scrum Master as quickly as possible to minimize cycle time impact.

name: Board View
definition: A visual display of work items organized in vertical columns, one per workflow step, allowing teams to see the state of all active work at a glance. Items can be dragged between columns to update their workflow step. Board view is the primary view on the In Progress page in Atono and supports filtering by assignee, size, and other fields.

name: Bug
definition: An error, flaw, or unintended behavior in software requiring investigation and resolution. Each bug includes a title, structured description (summary, reproduction steps, expected behavior, actual behavior), risk rating calculated from probability × impact (1–25 scale), affected environments, and an optional assignee. Bugs move through team workflows, participate in Bug Triage before team assignment, and support cycle time tracking, duplication, version history, threaded comments, linked items, subtasks, and attachments.

name: Bug Triage
definition: A workspace-wide page and process for evaluating newly reported bugs before they are assigned to a team. Bugs remain in triage until they receive a team assignment and a risk rating (calculated as probability × impact). The triage page is organized into Reported Bugs, More Info Required, and Won't Do sections. A badge indicates when bugs have been in triage for more than seven days without action.

name: Bulk Edit
definition: Simultaneously applying a change to multiple selected stories or bugs at once. Supported bulk edits in Atono include updating the assignee, workflow step, story size, product theme, timebox, and team. Bulk editing reduces repetitive work when reorganizing a backlog or updating a set of items after a scope decision.

name: Burndown Chart
definition: A Scrum team chart tracking remaining story points in a sprint over time, updated as stories are completed or moved out of scope. Plots actual remaining work against an ideal linear burn rate and projects sprint completion based on recent team velocity. Flags stories at risk of not finishing and surfaces items that could be pulled in if capacity allows. Available for active and completed sprints; the team's configured time zone is used for date grouping.

name: Burnup Chart
definition: A chart showing two lines over time: total work scope and total work completed. Unlike a burndown chart, which shows remaining work, a burnup chart makes scope changes visible as upward movement in the scope line. Burnup charts are useful when scope is expected to change during a sprint or release, as they clearly distinguish between work added and work completed.

name: Capacity
definition: The total available work effort a team has in a sprint or time period, typically expressed in story points or hours. Capacity accounts for team size, individual availability, holidays, and planned non-project time. Comparing planned sprint scope against capacity helps teams avoid overcommitting during sprint planning.

name: Change Request
definition: A formal or informal proposal to modify agreed-upon scope, requirements, or acceptance criteria after work has begun. Change requests can originate from stakeholders, product managers, or the team itself. Accepting a change request mid-sprint typically affects the sprint goal and may require removing other items to maintain realistic scope.

name: Collaborator
definition: A user who contributes to the work on a story or bug without being the primary assignee. Collaborators may comment, update acceptance criteria, add attachments, or move the item through workflow steps. In Atono, any team member with access to a team's stories can act as a collaborator. The assignee field tracks primary ownership while collaboration is open to all team members.

name: Comment
definition: A discussion thread on a story or bug, either on the item as a whole or anchored to specific highlighted text or an acceptance criterion. Supports @mentions (which trigger notifications), inline item ID references, and per-comment actions including accept, reject, edit, and delete. Open comments appear in a side panel; resolved comments are archived in Activities.

name: Continuous Integration
definition: A software development practice where team members merge code changes into a shared branch frequently, triggering automated builds and tests on each merge. Continuous integration surfaces integration errors quickly and reduces the risk of large, difficult merges. In Atono, pull requests linked to stories provide visibility into CI status as part of the development workflow.

name: Cumulative Flow Diagram
definition: A stacked area chart showing the count of work items in each workflow state over time. The width of each band represents the number of items in that state; a widening band indicates accumulating work-in-progress. The horizontal distance between the entry and exit of a band approximates cycle time. Cumulative flow diagrams help teams identify bottlenecks and monitor flow stability.

name: Customer
definition: A named tenant or organization whose users interact with your product, identified by a string value passed to the Atono SDK or Chrome extension. Used to scope feature flag slices to specific customers and to filter Feature Engagement graphs. Customer values appear in Slice conditions, usage tracking events, and Engagement page filters.

name: Cycle Time
definition: The total time a story or bug spends in workflow steps assigned to the In Progress category for a specific team. Cycle time begins when an item first enters an In Progress step and ends when it leaves the last In Progress step (entering Done or Won't do). Items with unusually long cycle times can be marked as outliers to exclude them from averages, staleness calculations, and estimated completion dates.

name: Cycle Time Report
definition: A team-specific report visualizing average cycle times for stories and bugs over a selected timeframe (1-60 months). Resolution adjusts automatically (daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly). Can be filtered by story size. Shows which data is used in cycle time calculations based on team configuration. Uses team time zone for date grouping.

name: Daily Standup
definition: A short, time-boxed synchronization meeting held each working day where team members share what they completed yesterday, what they plan to do today, and any blockers preventing progress. Also called a daily scrum in the Scrum framework. The standup is designed to improve team coordination and surface impediments early, not to provide status reports to management.

name: Data Export
definition: The process of downloading workspace data in a portable format for external analysis, archiving, or migration. Data exports may include stories, bugs, epics, comments, and workflow history. Exports are typically available in CSV or JSON format and are accessible to Workspace Owners and Administrators from workspace settings.

name: Definition of Done
definition: A shared team agreement listing all criteria that must be satisfied before a story or bug is considered complete. Common criteria include code reviewed, tests written, documentation updated, and acceptance criteria verified. The definition of done ensures consistent quality across the team and reduces ambiguity about when work can be marked as finished.

name: Definition of Ready
definition: A set of criteria a story must satisfy before a team can commit to working on it in a sprint or pull it from the backlog. Typical criteria include having clear acceptance criteria, an estimated size, and no unresolved dependencies. The definition of ready reduces mid-sprint surprises and helps teams start work with shared understanding.

name: Dependency
definition: A relationship where one work item cannot be started or completed until another item is finished. Dependencies create sequencing constraints and are a common source of sprint planning risk. Identifying dependencies early helps teams reorder work, negotiate delivery, or split stories to reduce blocking chains. In Atono, dependencies can be represented using linked items.

name: Deployment Pipeline
definition: An automated sequence of steps—such as build, test, and release—that code changes move through from commit to production. Deployment pipelines enforce quality gates and reduce the risk of releasing broken code. Feature flags in Atono complement deployment pipelines by enabling code to be deployed while keeping features disabled until they are ready for users.

name: Design Decisions
definition: An AI Context section capturing product and scope decisions made during story authoring, including tradeoffs, AC framing, scope boundaries, and deferred items. Updated when acceptance criteria changes reflect meaningful scope shifts. Reflects current product intent, not a running log.

name: Done (Workflow Category)
definition: A workflow category containing steps representing completed work. Items in Done steps do not contribute to cycle time. Each workflow must have at least one Done step. In the default workflow, the Done step is in this category.

name: Draft
definition: An unsaved version of a story or bug being created, displayed in a partial overlay that allows navigation while editing. Persists across browser refreshes and sign-outs. Only one draft can exist per user at a time, indicated by a badge on Create New when minimized.

name: Duplicate
definition: An action that creates a copy of a story or bug, prefixing its title with 'Copy of' and carrying over the description, risk rating (bugs), and size/product theme (stories). The copy's workflow step, assignee, backlog position, and timebox depend on the original item's workflow category. Items in To do or In Progress keep their team assignment; items in Done or Won't do are unassigned. The duplicate can optionally be linked to the original.

name: Engagement (Page)
definition: A workspace page showing a Top Features graph that ranks up to 10 features by usage across the product. Filterable by product theme, team, environment, completed date, customer, or location. Remembers user preferences for timeframe and filters.

name: Environment
definition: A named stage in the software development lifecycle such as dev, test, stage, or prod, used to manage feature flags and track bugs. Each environment has a unique key for SDK integration. Workspaces include four default environments that can be renamed, reordered, or deleted. New environments can be created by Workspace Owners and Administrators.

name: Environment Key
definition: A unique identifier for an environment, used to connect application code to Atono for feature flag evaluation and usage tracking. Displayed on the Environments page and copyable for use in the SDK.

name: Epic
definition: A planning item that groups related stories under a single shared goal, providing a high-level view of progress across multiple pieces of work. Epics include a title, one or more user stories defining intended outcomes, and a description. Stories can belong to only one epic at a time. When a story is split, both resulting stories remain in the original epic unless a new epic is created during the split. Epics appear in search results, the Everything page, and timelines.

name: Estimated Completion Date
definition: A forecast for when items in a Kanban team's To do category will be completed, based on the team's average cycle time and the current backlog queue order. Updates dynamically as items are reordered. Highlighted in orange when falling after an item's timebox end date. Not available for Scrum teams or outliers.

name: Estimation
definition: The process of predicting the relative effort required to complete a story or bug, typically expressed in story points using a Fibonacci scale. Estimation is performed collaboratively by the development team — often using planning poker — to surface differing assumptions and reach a shared understanding. Estimates feed into sprint planning, velocity calculations, and projected completion dates. In Atono, estimation is captured as the story size field (XXS through XXL), which maps to story points used in burndown charts and total story point counts.

name: Everything (Page)
definition: A centralized, filterable view of all stories, bugs, and epics in the workspace that the user has permission to view. Supports multi-criteria filtering, sorting, saved/shared views, column customization, and bulk actions (add to timebox/product theme). Title shows visible/total item counts. Private team items are excluded or redacted based on permissions.

name: Feature Engagement
definition: A story-level usage graph showing how often a feature is used, filterable by environment, customer, or location. Displays top 10 customers by default or compares usage across environments. Supports bar charts (totals) and line graphs (trends). Requires SDK-based usage tracking or mapped clicks via Chrome extension. Graph position moves to top of story when in Done category.

name: Feature Flag
definition: A named toggle controlling whether a feature is visible or active in each environment, without requiring a code deployment. Flags are created as standalone items or applied to stories. Each flag has a unique name (4–30 characters, lowercase letters and underscores), a description, an optional flag owner, and per-environment configurations that can target specific customers or locations via slices. The Atono SDK evaluates flags in real time and records the last evaluation timestamp and usage data.

name: Feature Request
definition: A suggestion from a user, customer, or stakeholder for new or improved product functionality. Feature requests are the raw material of product discovery and are evaluated against user needs, strategic goals, and delivery capacity before being accepted into the backlog. In Atono, feature requests are typically captured as stories in Story Refinement, where they are refined, sized, and prioritized before being assigned to a team backlog and sprint.

name: Filter
definition: Criteria applied to narrow the set of visible items in backlogs, the Everything page, timelines, or reports. Filters can target fields including assignee, team, product theme, story size, workflow step, epic, sprint, timebox, risk rating, and more. Multiple filters combine with AND logic. Active filters persist within a session and can be saved as a Saved View for future reuse.

name: Flag Configuration
definition: The settings controlling a feature flag's behavior in each environment. Includes ON/OFF state and optional slices defining conditions based on customer or location attributes. Configuration changes are versioned and appear in Activities. Slices are evaluated top-to-bottom with first-match logic.

name: Flag Evaluation
definition: The process by which the Atono SDK determines whether a feature flag is ON or OFF for a given user, customer, or location at runtime. During evaluation, the SDK checks the flag's per-environment configuration and applies any defined slices to return a boolean result. The timestamp of the most recent evaluation is recorded and visible in the flag's status panel.

name: Flag Owner
definition: A permission level granting full control over a feature flag including configuration, naming, permissions management, removal from stories, and deletion. Automatically assigned to the flag creator. Cannot be the sole owner removed from a flag.

name: Flag Permissions
definition: Access controls for feature flags, separate from workspace and team roles. Flag User can modify configuration and toggle flags. Flag Owner has User permissions plus ability to edit name/description, manage permissions, remove from stories, and delete flags. Creator is automatically assigned Owner. Teams are auto-assigned User permissions when a flagged story is added to their backlog if no team currently has permissions.

name: Flag Status
definition: A summary of a feature flag's current configuration and evaluation state across all stories and environments. Possible statuses: Not Yet Evaluated, Currently Off, Mixed Configuration, Currently On, No Recent Evaluations (>30 days). Used to determine if a flag can be safely deleted.

name: Flag Targeting
definition: Rules within a flag's per-environment configuration that determine which customers or locations see the flag as enabled. Targeting uses slice definitions with operators (is, is not, contains, is one of, is in, is not in) to evaluate customer identifiers and location data. When no targeting rules match, the flag falls back to the environment's default ON/OFF state.

name: Flag User
definition: A permission level allowing configuration and toggling of feature flags on/off and defining slices, but not editing flag name/description, managing permissions, removing from stories, or deleting. Cannot be the last User removed from a flag.

name: Flow Efficiency
definition: The ratio of active work time to total lead time for a work item, expressed as a percentage. Flow efficiency reveals how much of an item's lifespan is spent in active development versus waiting (in queue, blocked, or idle). High flow efficiency indicates minimal waste; low flow efficiency points to queuing or blocking problems. A team average above 15% is considered good in most software contexts.

name: Follow
definition: An action marking a story or bug (via star icon) so it appears in filtered views and dashboard widgets. Following is user-specific and doesn't affect the item or other users.

name: GitHub Integration
definition: A workspace integration connecting Atono to a GitHub organization, enabling automatic linking of pull requests to stories and bugs. Pull requests are linked when the story or bug ID appears in the PR title, description, branch name, or optionally in comments and commit messages. Requires a Workspace Owner or Administrator to configure.

name: Home (Page)
definition: A user's landing page in Atono showing widgets for assigned work, followed items, and any saved/shared views added as widgets. Includes shortcuts to invite users, join/create teams, and connect Slack. Widgets can be added, removed, and reordered per user.

name: Identity Provider
definition: An external system managing user identities and authentication for an organization, used with SSO. When SSO is enabled, Atono delegates authentication to the identity provider and confirms email addresses through it.

name: Impact
definition: The severity of a bug's effect on users, rated 1-5 (Negligible to Severe) during Bug Triage. Multiplied by probability to calculate the bug's risk rating (1-25 scale). Can be manually set or suggested by Atono.

name: Import
definition: The process of bringing existing work from Jira or Linear into Atono, including issues, teams, projects, and optionally users. Issues become stories or bugs with preserved metadata (dates, assignments, descriptions). Teams are matched by name or created. External IDs preserve original issue keys.

name: In Progress (Page)
definition: A team page showing active work moving through workflow steps. For Scrum teams, displays only the active sprint. For Kanban teams, shows the 20 most recent To do items plus all work in progress. Supports list/board views, filters, staleness indicators, and optional display of pull requests, product themes, timeboxes, and total story points.

name: In Progress (Workflow Category)
definition: A workflow category containing steps representing active work. Time spent in these steps contributes to an item's cycle time. The default workflow includes Design, Development, Test, and Review in this category. Each workflow must have at least one In Progress step for stories or bugs.

name: Incident
definition: An unplanned interruption or significant reduction in the quality of a production service. Incidents range from complete outages to degraded performance and typically require immediate investigation and resolution. In Atono, incidents are tracked as bugs with a high risk rating, using the affected environment field to indicate the impacted deployment stage and the description to capture reproduction steps, impact scope, and timeline. A postmortem may follow to prevent recurrence.

name: Increment
definition: A completed, potentially shippable piece of product output delivered at the end of each sprint. The increment includes all work completed in the current sprint plus all previous increments, representing the current state of the product. In Scrum, every sprint must produce an increment, even if it is not released to customers immediately.

name: Inline Comment
definition: A comment anchored to a specific word, phrase, or passage within a story's user story text, acceptance criteria, or additional content. Inline comments surface in context alongside the highlighted text, making it easy to discuss specific details without losing their location. Resolved inline comments are archived in Activities.

name: Invite
definition: The process of adding a new user to an Atono workspace by sending them an email invitation containing a join link. Invitations are sent by Workspace Owners or Administrators. The invited user must accept the invitation and create or sign in to an Atono account. Once accepted, the user is added to the workspace with the Standard User role by default.

name: Item ID
definition: A unique identifier assigned to each story, bug, or epic in the format TYPE-NUMBER (e.g., STORY-123, BUG-456, EPIC-789). Assigned sequentially; numbers are not reused unless the deleted item was the next available ID. Used for linking, searching, and referencing items.

name: Iteration
definition: A fixed-length time-boxed development cycle used to deliver a defined set of work. In Scrum, iterations are called sprints. In other agile frameworks, iteration length and structure vary. The term is often used interchangeably with sprint in Atono contexts.

name: Kanban
definition: A team workflow type focused on continuous flow rather than time-boxed iterations. Kanban teams pull work from a 'To do' queue into active workflow steps, aiming to minimize work in progress and maximize throughput. Estimated completion dates are calculated from average cycle time and backlog queue position. Kanban teams do not have sprints, velocity, or burndown charts.

name: Key Result
definition: A specific, measurable outcome used to track progress toward an OKR objective. Key results are quantitative (e.g., increase feature adoption by 20%) and have a defined target. Teams map their stories and epics to key results to ensure delivery work connects to measurable business outcomes.

name: Keyboard Shortcut
definition: A key or combination of keys that triggers a specific action in Atono without using the mouse. Keyboard shortcuts speed up common operations like opening search, creating a new story, navigating between pages, and submitting forms. A full list of shortcuts is available from the help menu within the application.

name: Kill Switch
definition: A feature flag configured specifically to provide an immediate, reliable way to disable a feature across all users in any environment without a code deployment. Kill switches are a safety mechanism for production incidents, allowing teams to turn off a misbehaving feature in seconds. Best practice is to keep kill switches separate from flags used for gradual rollouts or A/B testing.

name: Lead Time
definition: The total elapsed time from when a work item is created or requested to when it is fully delivered (moved to Done). Lead time encompasses both waiting time in the backlog and active work time in In Progress steps. It is a broader measure than cycle time, which counts only active work time. Reducing lead time is a key goal of lean and agile delivery practices.

name: Linked Items
definition: Stories, bugs, or epics explicitly connected to each other to show relationships like dependencies or related work. Links are bidirectional and visible in the Linked Items section of each item. Can include suggested items based on content similarity. Does not include inline item ID mentions, which are clickable but not formal links.

name: List View
definition: A tabular display of work items with one item per row and configurable columns such as title, assignee, size, workflow step, and product theme. List view supports sorting by any column and is optimized for bulk review and planning. The Backlog page and Everything page both offer a list view alongside other display options.

name: Location
definition: A geographic attribute derived from a user's IP address at the time of a feature flag evaluation or usage tracking event. Used as a slice condition in feature flag configurations to enable or disable features for users in specific regions. Atono automatically detects location without requiring explicit values from the SDK caller.

name: Mention
definition: Tagging a specific user in a comment or description by typing @ followed by their name or email. A mention sends the tagged user a notification and creates a link to their profile. Mentions are supported in story and bug comments and in the description fields. Mentioned users are notified by email.

name: Milestone
definition: A significant checkpoint marking a key delivery or decision point in a product plan, such as a public launch, a regulatory deadline, or a major feature release. In Atono, milestones are represented as release markers on a timeline, providing a visual anchor for planning and communicating delivery commitments.

name: Notification
definition: An email message sent to a user when a relevant event occurs in Atono. Notification triggers include: being assigned to a story or bug, being @mentioned in a comment, a comment being accepted or rejected, a story or bug being moved to Done. Notifications are sent to the user's registered email address. Users can adjust which events generate notifications in their account settings.

name: Notification Settings
definition: User-configurable preferences controlling which Atono events generate email notifications. Users can enable or disable notifications for events such as being assigned work, receiving an @mention, or having a comment accepted or rejected. Notification settings are managed from the user's account settings page.

name: OAuth
definition: An open authorization standard that allows third-party applications to access Atono resources on behalf of a user without requiring the user to share their credentials. OAuth is used by external integrations and tools that need delegated access to workspace data. Atono supports OAuth for specific integration scenarios where API key authentication is not sufficient.

name: OKR
definition: Objectives and Key Results, a goal-setting framework linking qualitative objectives (what to achieve) to measurable key results (how success is measured). OKRs are typically set at quarterly or annual cadences at the company, team, and individual level. Teams use OKRs to align agile delivery work with strategic outcomes, often mapping epics or product themes to specific key results.

name: Outlier
definition: A story or bug with unusually long cycle time, marked to exclude it from average cycle time calculations, cycle time reports, staleness indicators, and estimated completion dates. Outlier status can be added or removed by Workspace Owners, Administrators, Backlog Owners, and Team Administrators. Preserves the item's data for reference.

name: Parking Lot
definition: A temporary holding area for ideas, topics, or work items that are raised during a meeting or planning session but are not immediately actionable or in scope. Items in the parking lot are noted for future consideration rather than ignored. In Atono, the parking lot concept is often implemented using the Won't do workflow category or Story Refinement for ideas not yet ready to be committed.

name: Persona
definition: A named user type in a user story's 'As a [persona]...' statement. Atono suggests previously-used personas during story creation. Available personas include those from stories in To do/In progress and from stories in Done within the last 90 days. Retrieved via MCP tools for AI assistants.

name: Planning Poker
definition: A consensus-based estimation technique where team members simultaneously reveal effort estimates for a story using cards or a digital tool. Differences in estimates prompt discussion, surfacing assumptions and risks that might otherwise go unnoticed. After discussion, the team re-estimates until they reach agreement. Planning poker encourages collaborative sizing rather than anchoring to a single person's estimate.

name: Portfolio
definition: A collection of epics, product themes, or strategic initiatives representing a major investment area for the organization. Portfolio management involves prioritizing and sequencing these larger units of work to allocate team capacity toward the highest-value opportunities. In Atono, portfolios are approximated through product themes and timeline views spanning multiple teams.

name: Postmortem
definition: A structured retrospective analysis conducted after a production incident to understand what went wrong, why it happened, and how to prevent recurrence. Blameless postmortems focus on systemic causes rather than individual mistakes and produce concrete action items such as new tests, monitoring improvements, or process changes. In Atono, postmortem action items are typically captured as bugs or technical investigation stories linked to the original incident bug.

name: Priority
definition: A relative ranking of work items indicating their order of importance or urgency. In Atono, priority is expressed through backlog ordering: items higher in the backlog queue are expected to be worked on sooner. Teams can also use product themes or labels to signal strategic priority. Backlog owners are responsible for maintaining priority order when backlog management is enabled.

name: Private Team
definition: A team with restricted visibility where only team members, Team Administrators, Backlog Owners, and Workspace Owners/Administrators can see the team's backlog and work items. Non-members see the team name but not its contents. Team members of a private team can manage membership. Contrast with public teams, which any workspace user can access.

name: Probability
definition: The likelihood that a user will encounter a bug, rated 1-5 (Impossible to Certain) during Bug Triage. Multiplied by impact to calculate the bug's risk rating (1-25 scale). Can be manually set or suggested by Atono.

name: Product Backlog
definition: An ordered list of all work known to be needed in the product, maintained and prioritized by the product owner. The product backlog is never complete — it evolves as the product and its context change. Higher-priority items are more refined and ready to work on; lower-priority items may be rough ideas. In Atono, the product backlog is managed through a combination of the team Backlog page and Story Refinement, where ideas are captured and prepared before being committed to a team.

name: Product Discovery
definition: The process of identifying, validating, and scoping user problems before committing to building a solution. Discovery activities include user interviews, prototype testing, data analysis, and competitive research. Discovery outputs—such as validated problem statements, personas, and feature hypotheses—feed directly into story authoring, acceptance criteria, and product themes in Atono.

name: Product Goal
definition: A long-term, outcome-oriented objective that provides direction for the product team's work across multiple sprints or releases. The product goal describes a future state of the product that delivers value to users or the business. In Scrum, the product goal is the current strategic objective the product backlog serves. Teams often align product themes and epics with active product goals.

name: Product Knowledge
definition: A feature that analyzes help documentation and builds a structured glossary of up to 200 key product concepts including name, description, synonyms, and relationships. Exported as TSV for use with AI tools. Managed by Glossary Contributors and Owners with permissions, version history, and conflict detection.

name: Product Manager
definition: A workspace role controlling who can manage product themes. When assigned in a workspace, Product Managers can add/remove themes, manage backlog owners for any team, access all public and private team backlogs, and manage glossary concepts. If no Product Managers are assigned, any user can manage product themes.

name: Product Owner
definition: The Scrum role responsible for maximizing the value of the product by managing, ordering, and communicating the contents of the product backlog. The product owner is the single accountable person for backlog priority decisions and ensures the team always works on the highest-value items. In Atono, the product owner's responsibilities are typically held by a user with the Backlog Owner team role and the Product Manager workspace role, giving them control over backlog ordering, sprint planning, and product themes.

name: Product Theme
definition: A named grouping for related stories aligning work with strategic goals and tracking long-term progress. Created and managed by Product Managers if that role is assigned; otherwise by anyone. Stories can belong to only one theme. Themes appear on backlogs, timelines, and in filters.

name: Projected Completion
definition: An estimated date when a story or bug will be completed, calculated differently for Kanban and Scrum teams. For Kanban teams in To do or In progress, based on current position, team cycle time, and daily throughput. For Scrum teams, reflects sprint timing (unscheduled or sprint end date). Warnings appear when projected date falls after timebox end date.

name: Pull Request
definition: A proposed code change in a Git-based version control system, automatically linked to Atono stories or bugs when the Atono item ID is mentioned in the PR title, description, or branch name. With the GitHub integration, PR mentions in comments and commit messages can also be configured to auto-link. Linked pull requests appear in the story or bug's Activities and in the Linked Items section, showing status (open, merged, closed) and a direct link to the PR.

name: Rate Limit
definition: A cap on the number of API requests a single user or API key can make to Atono within a defined time window. Rate limiting protects platform stability and ensures fair resource distribution across all workspace users. Requests exceeding the limit receive an HTTP 429 Too Many Requests response and should be retried with exponential backoff.

name: Regression
definition: An unintended failure in previously working functionality caused by a new code change. Regressions are a common source of bugs and are particularly risky after large refactors or releases touching shared code. Automated regression test suites and thorough acceptance criteria help teams catch regressions before they reach production.

name: Release
definition: A key delivery milestone represented by a date, created as release markers on timelines. Release names must be unique within a timeline. Releases can be surfaced in team backlogs: Scrum backlogs show releases as horizontal rows between sprints; Kanban backlogs let you select one release to highlight items projected to complete within that window.

name: Release Notes
definition: A document summarizing the new features, improvements, and bug fixes included in a product release, typically published when a release goes live. Release notes help users and stakeholders understand what has changed and why, reducing support burden and setting expectations. In Atono, the content for release notes is often sourced from completed stories and bugs linked to a specific release or timebox, using the release description and story acceptance criteria as the source of truth.

name: Retrospective
definition: A recurring ceremony held at the end of each sprint where the Scrum team reflects on how they worked together and identifies specific improvements to carry into the next sprint. Typical formats ask what went well, what could be improved, and what actions to take. Retrospectives are a core Scrum event focused on continuous team improvement rather than product output.

name: Risk
definition: A potential future event or condition that could negatively affect the delivery or quality of a story, sprint, or release. Risks differ from issues in that they have not yet occurred. Teams commonly identify and track risks during sprint planning or retrospectives. In Atono, bug risk is quantified as a risk rating (probability × impact), but broader project risks are typically discussed in team meetings or tracked in story descriptions.

name: Risk Rating
definition: A numerical assessment of a bug's overall risk, calculated by multiplying probability (1-5) by impact (1-5) to produce a value from 1-25, where 25 is highest risk. Can be manually set or suggested by Atono based on bug description. Required along with team assignment for a bug to move out of Bug Triage.

name: Roadmap
definition: A strategic visual plan showing the planned sequence and timing of product work across epics, releases, and themes. Roadmaps help teams and stakeholders understand what is being built, in what order, and why. In Atono, high-level roadmapping is supported through product themes, epics, timelines, and timeboxes.

name: Roles and Permissions
definition: Atono's multi-level access control system including workspace roles (Workspace Owner, Administrator, Product Manager, Standard User), team roles (Team Member, Team Administrator, Backlog Owner), and separate feature flag and glossary permissions. Roles can stack to grant combined privileges.

name: Rollout
definition: A controlled, gradual release of a feature to an increasing subset of users, customers, or environments. Rollouts reduce the risk of a full release by allowing teams to monitor behavior and catch issues before exposing a feature to all users. In Atono, rollouts are implemented by configuring feature flag slices to target specific customers or locations and expanding the targeting over time.

name: SAML
definition: Security Assertion Markup Language, a standard for exchanging authentication information between an identity provider and service provider. Atono supports SAML 2.0 for single sign-on on paid plans.

name: Saved View
definition: A named, personal collection of filters, sorting, and column customizations on the Everything page that can be returned to without reapplying settings. Can be shared with users or teams, and added as widgets to the Home page. View names must be unique per user (up to 100 characters).

name: Scope
definition: The defined set of features, stories, or work committed to for a sprint, epic, release, or project. Scope sets expectations for what will and will not be delivered in a given timeframe. Scope changes mid-sprint are discouraged in Scrum; Atono warns teams before allowing sprint scope modifications. Scope is often discussed alongside capacity to determine whether commitments are realistic.

name: Scrum
definition: A team workflow type focused on time-boxed iterations called sprints. Scrum teams plan using story points and velocity, with team pages including Backlog, In Progress, and Sprint Calendar. Supports burndown charts, velocity tracking, and sprint-based planning.

name: Scrum Master
definition: The Scrum team role responsible for facilitating Scrum ceremonies, coaching the team on Scrum practices, and removing impediments that block progress. The Scrum Master is a servant-leader who protects the team from outside distractions, ensures the sprint goal stays visible, and drives continuous improvement through retrospectives. Scrum Master is not a formal permission role in Atono but is typically the team member who manages sprint cadence, maintains the burndown chart, and coordinates with stakeholders during sprint reviews.

name: SDK
definition: Software Development Kit, a set of tools and libraries from Atono for integrating feature flags and usage tracking into applications. Available for web and React with client-side support. Requires environment key for initialization. Version 1.1.0+ required for usage tracking.

name: Search
definition: A global fuzzy keyword search across all stories, bugs, and epics the user has permission to view. Search matches against titles, item IDs, descriptions, user stories, acceptance criteria, and active (unresolved) comments. Results are ranked by relevance. Search is accessible from the top navigation bar and can be used alongside filters on the Everything page for more precise queries.

name: Severity
definition: The degree of impact a bug has on users or core system functionality, independent of how likely users are to encounter it. Severity is distinct from probability: a crash on a rare code path is high severity but low probability. In Atono, severity is captured as part of the impact field during bug triage, contributing to the overall risk rating.

name: Shared View
definition: A saved view that has been shared with specific users or teams, appearing in their shared views list. Can be re-shared by recipients. Original owner can remove any shares. Shared views remember filters and sorting but not column selections.

name: Slack Integration
definition: A workspace integration connecting Atono to a Slack workspace, enabling automated updates and communication across both platforms. Capabilities include: creating dedicated Slack channels for teams and individual stories, posting updates when team settings or feature flag configurations change, and connecting individual Atono user accounts to their Slack identities for mention routing. Configured by an Administrator from Workspace Settings.

name: Slice
definition: A defined subset within an environment for a feature flag, based on customer and/or location attributes with specific operators (is, is not, contains, is one of, is in, is not in). Each slice has an ON/OFF value. Evaluated top-to-bottom with first-match logic; default value used if no slice matches. A slice can have up to two conditions (one customer, one location).

name: Snooze
definition: An action that temporarily hides the staleness indicator for a specific item in a specific workflow step for a chosen duration (1 day, 1 week, 1 month, or until changed). Only available to team admins and workspace Administrators. Resets if the item changes steps or teams.

name: Split
definition: An action dividing a story into two stories by moving selected acceptance criteria into a new story. Preserves team, product theme, timebox, and feature flags on both stories. AC parents may be moved or copied based on children selection. Both stories are automatically linked. Available only to users with appropriate permissions on managed backlogs.

name: Sprint
definition: A time-boxed iteration for Scrum teams, typically one to four weeks, with defined start and end dates and a committed set of stories working toward a sprint goal. Each team can have one active sprint and unlimited future sprints planned. Sprint scope can be adjusted mid-sprint with a warning. Completed sprints contribute to velocity calculations. Sprint history is visible in the Sprint Calendar.

name: Sprint Backlog
definition: The set of product backlog items selected for a sprint, combined with the team's plan for delivering them toward the sprint goal. The sprint backlog is owned by the development team and is updated daily as work progresses. Items can be added or removed with caution, and Atono warns when sprint scope is modified after the sprint starts. The sprint backlog is visible on the In Progress and Backlog pages during an active sprint.

name: Sprint Calendar
definition: A Scrum-only team page providing a visual schedule of past, active, and future sprints. Shows sprint names, item counts, story point totals, and projected capacity for future sprints. Supports scrolling, zooming (days to 5 years), resizing sprint height, creating/editing/deleting sprints, and viewing sprint details including burndown charts.

name: Sprint Goal
definition: A concise objective that the Scrum team commits to achieving by the end of a sprint. The sprint goal gives the sprint a unified purpose and helps the team make decisions when work needs to be adjusted mid-sprint. Having a sprint goal distinct from individual story completion encourages teams to think about outcomes rather than outputs.

name: Sprint Planning
definition: A Scrum ceremony at the start of each sprint where the team selects stories from the backlog and commits to a sprint goal. The team reviews capacity, estimates remaining uncertainty, and agrees on which items can realistically be completed. In Atono, sprint planning is done from the Backlog page and may be restricted to backlog owners when Backlog Management is enabled.

name: Sprint Review
definition: A Scrum ceremony held at the end of each sprint where the team demonstrates completed work to stakeholders and gathers feedback. The sprint review provides an opportunity to inspect the increment and adapt the product backlog based on what was learned. It is distinct from the retrospective, which focuses on team process rather than product output.

name: SSO
definition: Single sign-on, an authentication method allowing users to sign in to Atono using their organization's identity provider instead of email-based login links. Supports SAML 2.0 for workspaces on paid plans. Three modes: On (all users), Off (no users), Testing (Owners and Administrators only).

name: Stakeholder
definition: A user with a critical team role (Team Administrator or Backlog Owner) who is not a team member. Displayed in a separate Stakeholders section on the Team Overview page for visibility.

name: Staleness Indicator
definition: A red hourglass icon appearing on stories or bugs in In Progress steps when time spent exceeds 3 standard deviations of the team's average for similar items in that step. Requires at least 5 historical items for calculation. Excludes outliers. Can be snoozed by team admins and workspace Administrators. Resets when item changes steps or teams.

name: Standard User
definition: The default workspace role assigned to all users, providing typical functionality including creating teams, managing public team backlogs (without backlog owners), creating/managing bugs and stories on public teams, and using most Atono features. Can gain additional privileges through team roles or feature flag permissions.

name: Story
definition: The core unit of user-focused work in Atono, capturing what needs to be built and why. Each story includes a title, one or more user stories in 'As a / I want to / So that' format, hierarchical acceptance criteria, an optional additional context section, story size, product theme, assignee, epic, timebox, and linked feature flags. Stories move through a team's workflow steps, track cycle time, support version history, comments, attachments, subtasks, and linked items, and can be duplicated or split into multiple stories.

name: Story Map
definition: A two-dimensional visual arrangement of user stories organized by user journey across the horizontal axis and priority or detail level down the vertical axis. Story maps help teams see the complete user experience, identify gaps, and plan incremental releases. The top row represents the high-level journey steps (backbone); lower rows contain the detailed stories that support each step.

name: Story Points
definition: A numerical measure derived from story size (XXS=1, XS=2, S=3, M=5, L=8, XL=13, XXL=21) using Fibonacci numbers. Unsized stories are assigned the nearest Fibonacci number to the team's average. Used for sprint capacity planning, burndown charts, and velocity tracking in Scrum teams.

name: Story Refinement
definition: A workspace-wide backlog for capturing ideas and preparing stories before assigning them to teams. Has fixed workflow steps (Idea, Refinement, Ready for Assignment, Won't Do) accessible to all users. Does not track sizing, estimated completion dates, cycle time, or staleness.

name: Story Size
definition: A relative measure of effort to complete a story: Unsized, XXS, XS, S, M, L, XL, XXL. Can be manually set or suggested by Atono based on similar completed stories (requires 9+ sized stories in 3+ sizes). Converted to story points for planning. Cleared when story is reassigned to a different team.

name: Subtask
definition: An implementation step within a story or bug with title, optional description (up to 1000 chars with rich text), assignee, and status (To do, In progress, Done, Won't do). Displayed with a progress meter excluding Won't do items. Changes are logged in Activities with dedicated filter. Can be reordered by dragging. Not available on drafts.

name: Team
definition: A group of users organized to plan and deliver work together in Atono, using either Scrum or Kanban workflow. Each team has its own workflow, backlog, members, 1+ team admins, optional backlog owners, and can be public or private. Limited to 50 teams per workspace, 50 members per team. Has optional time zone setting for consistent reporting.

name: Team Administrator
definition: A team role with permissions to edit team details, delete team, modify access type and methodology, manage team admins and backlog owners, customize workflow, and connect Slack channels. Does not need to be a team member. Cannot remove themselves as admin; at least one admin must remain.

name: Team Member
definition: A team role granted automatically when a user joins a public or private team. Can edit team name/description, connect/remove Slack channels, and (for private teams) access the team backlog and manage team membership.

name: Team Overview (Page)
definition: A team page showing general information about a team including its name, description, methodology (Scrum or Kanban), access type (public or private), members, and stakeholders. Stakeholders — users with a critical team role (Team Administrator or Backlog Owner) who are not team members — appear in a dedicated section for visibility.

name: Team Role
definition: Permissions granted within a specific team (Team Member, Team Administrator, Backlog Owner) that do not affect workspace-level access. Allow users to manage teams without requiring workspace-wide Administrator privileges.

name: Technical Changes
definition: An AI Context section recording current implementation changes made by AI tools for a story or bug. Includes what was added/changed/removed, test updates, behavior changes, and outcomes. Reflects current state; reverted changes should be removed. Also called SUMMARY in MCP contexts.

name: Technical Debt
definition: Accumulated shortcuts, deferred improvements, and suboptimal design decisions in a codebase that require future rework. Technical debt may be intentional (a deliberate shortcut to meet a deadline) or unintentional (poor design discovered later). Left unaddressed, technical debt slows future development and increases the risk of bugs. Teams often track technical debt as stories or bugs with a dedicated product theme.

name: Technical Investigation
definition: An AI Context section capturing discoveries, research findings, and constraints gathered during AI-assisted implementation. Includes findings, relevant files/endpoints/dependencies, open questions, and recommended next steps. Called INVESTIGATION in MCP contexts.

name: Throughput
definition: The number of work items a team completes per unit of time, regardless of their size or complexity. Throughput is a flow-based metric often used alongside cycle time to understand team delivery rate. Unlike velocity, throughput does not weight items by story points, making it useful for teams that prefer not to estimate. In Kanban, throughput is a primary indicator of team performance.

name: Timebox
definition: A named, date-bounded container grouping stories and bugs into a defined delivery window. Can contain up to 100 items, appear on multiple timelines, and show warnings when items may not complete by end date. Displayed on timelines with visual indicators for themes, teams, and delivery risks. Detail view shows item-level projected completion and total story points if all items belong to one team.

name: Timeline
definition: A visual schedule of timeboxes created and owned by a user, showing how work is organized over time. Supports scrolling, zooming (days to 5 years), filtering by team/product theme, release markers, and timebox management (add, reorder, resize, color, remove). Displays warnings for timeboxes ending within 3 months that have delivery risks.

name: To do (Workflow Category)
definition: A workflow category containing one step representing work ready to start but not yet active. Time in To do steps does not contribute to cycle time. Required for all workflows. In the default workflow, the To do step is in this category.

name: Total Story Points
definition: The combined Fibonacci-based size of all visible stories in a column, sprint, or timebox, including an estimated size for unsized stories based on team average. Appears on Scrum backlogs (sprint headers), In Progress pages (workflow steps), Sprint Calendar (future sprints with projected capacity), Everything page (when filtered to one team), and timelines/timeboxes (when all items belong to one team). Updates dynamically as stories are added, removed, or resized.

name: Usage Tracking
definition: Recording user interactions with features to power Feature Engagement graphs. Implemented by calling SDK record() methods in code or by mapping clicks via the Atono Chrome extension. Requires SDK v1.1.0+ running client-side. Events include story ID, environment, optional customer, and auto-detected location from IP.

name: User Feedback
definition: Input gathered from users through interviews, surveys, support tickets, usage analytics, or direct observation that reveals how people interact with the product and what problems they experience. User feedback drives feature requests, informs acceptance criteria, and validates product decisions. In Atono, insights from user feedback are typically documented in story descriptions or design decisions sections and may be linked to customer data visible through feature engagement and usage tracking.

name: User Profile
definition: A page showing user details including email, name, workspace role, and team memberships. Users can edit their own name, connect Slack, manage API keys, and configure notification preferences (email for assignments). Accessible from the workspace header menu.

name: User Story
definition: A brief, structured description of a feature from an end user's perspective, written in the format: 'As a [persona], I want to [action] so that [benefit].' Each story or epic can contain one or more user stories. The persona identifies the type of user; the action describes the desired capability; the benefit explains the underlying motivation or outcome. User stories guide acceptance criteria authoring and help teams stay focused on user value.

name: Velocity
definition: The average number of story points a Scrum team completes per sprint, calculated using only the team's most recent completed sprints within the configured velocity averaging window. Teams with fewer completed sprints than the window size use all available sprints. Velocity is used to project burndown chart completion, estimate future sprint capacity, and calculate estimated completion dates for the Kanban backlog queue.

name: Velocity Averaging Window
definition: A team-level setting controlling how many recent completed sprints are used to calculate velocity. A shorter window reacts faster to recent performance; a longer window smooths out outlier sprints. Configured by Team Administrators and affects sprint capacity projections on the Sprint Calendar and Backlog.

name: Velocity Chart
definition: A bar chart showing the number of story points a Scrum team completed in each past sprint, used to visualize trends in team throughput over time. The velocity chart helps teams identify whether they are improving, declining, or maintaining consistent output, and informs capacity planning for future sprints.

name: Version History
definition: A record of all changes to a story or bug's content (title, user stories, acceptance criteria, description) showing additions (green) and deletions (red strikethrough). Multiple edits within a short timeframe are grouped. Previous versions can be restored, creating a new version. AI Context has separate version history per section.

name: Watcher
definition: A user who follows a story or bug to receive notifications about changes to that item without being the primary assignee. In Atono, following an item (via the star icon) acts as the watch mechanism, causing the item to appear in the user's followed items widget and personal filters. This is called Follow in Atono.

name: Webhook
definition: An HTTP callback that sends real-time event data from Atono to an external URL when a specified event occurs, such as a story being created, updated, or moved to a new workflow step. Webhooks enable integrations with external systems like CI/CD pipelines, dashboards, or custom automation tools without requiring the external system to poll Atono continuously.

name: Widget
definition: A customizable panel on the Home page displaying items from a saved or shared view. Users can add widgets for any view, remove them, and rearrange them. Default widgets include Assigned to Me and Following.

name: WIP Limit
definition: A maximum number of work items permitted to be simultaneously active in a single workflow step or across the entire In Progress category. WIP limits are a core Kanban practice that force teams to finish work before starting new items, reducing context switching and identifying process bottlenecks. In Atono, workflow steps can be configured with WIP limits visible on the In Progress page.

name: Won't do (Workflow Category)
definition: A workflow category containing one step for work that will not be completed. Items in Won't do do not appear in some views, don't contribute to cycle time, velocity, or burndown charts, and are excluded from staleness indicators. Each workflow must have exactly one Won't do step.

name: Work Item
definition: A generic term encompassing any story, bug, or epic in Atono. Work item is used in contexts where a statement applies equally to all three types, such as search, linked items, the Everything page, and API documentation. Each work item has a unique item ID in the format TYPE-NUMBER.

name: Workflow
definition: A customizable sequence of 4–25 named steps that stories and bugs move through from ready-to-start to completion. Each step belongs to one of four workflow categories: To do, In Progress, Done, or Won't do. Steps have a name and color. Only one step can exist in the To do category and one in the Won't do category; the In Progress and Done categories can have multiple steps. Cycle time is measured across all In Progress steps. Teams can reorder steps and rename them; the workflow is shared across all team members.

name: Workflow Category
definition: One of four organizational groupings for workflow steps: To do (1 step), In Progress (1+ steps, contributes to cycle time), Done (1+ steps), Won't do (1 step). Categories standardize metrics across teams despite workflow customization. Cannot be empty of applicable steps for stories or bugs.

name: Workflow Step
definition: A named stage in a workflow with assigned color and category (To do, In progress, Done, Won't do). Can be configured to apply to stories, bugs, or both. Steps can be reordered, renamed, added (up to 25 total), deleted (with reassignment), and moved between categories by team admins and workspace Administrators.

name: Workspace
definition: The top-level container for all teams, stories, bugs, users, and other elements in Atono for a company. Each workspace has a unique URL (example.atono.io), name, up to 50 teams, and plan-based limits for users and storage. Created during initial signup with the creator as Workspace Owner.

name: Workspace Owner
definition: A workspace role with highest-level permissions including all Administrator capabilities plus managing plans, billing, adding/removing other Workspace Owners, and deleting the workspace. Cannot remove their own role or be deleted. Automatically assigned to workspace creator.

name: Workspace Role
definition: A primary permission level defining system-wide access: Workspace Owner (highest), Administrator (full admin without billing), Product Manager (theme/glossary management), or Standard User (default). Separate from team roles and feature flag permissions.
